commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2bc06a19a8e868f2f395f6aea3f62851d6dac92f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `deliveryThreadId` fallback to routed thread resolution and wires ACP session `deliveryContext.threadId` into prepared-reply and dispatch-from-config routes behind an ACP session-key guard.

PR surface: Source +31. Total +31 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main’s `mode="session"` ACP spawn shape carries `spawnedBy`, current ACP policy suppresses parent-owned child user delivery, and ACP delivery returns before routing when suppressed. I did not run a live Discord reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the patch has a concrete delivery-suppression blocker and still lacks contributor real-environment proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: fix: resolve ACP delivery threadId from session deliveryContext
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ACP sessions (e.g. Discord delivery via ACP) fail to route replies because `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` cannot extract a `threadId` from ACP session keys. The function's three-tier fallback chain — `MessageThreadId` → `TransportThreadId` → `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` — all return nothing because:

1. `MessageThreadId` and `TransportThreadId` are null when delivering through the ACP coordinator
2. `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` parses thread info from `:thread:` suffix, but ACP session keys use `agent:<id>:acp:<uuid>` format

However, the session entry's `deliveryContext.threadId` IS already set during ACP spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` adds it via `requestDeliveryHint`), and `preparedSessionState.sessionEntry` is available at the call site. The data was there — it just wasn't being used.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix adds a `deliveryThreadId` parameter to `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` that slots between the inbound thread id checks and the session-key parse:

```
MessageThreadId → TransportThreadId → deliveryThreadId (NEW) → parseSessionThreadInfoFast
```

Placed before `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` because `deliveryContext.threadId` is authoritatively set by the gateway during spawn and is more reliable than parsing session key conventions.

Only the `get-reply-run.ts` call site is wired up (the entry point that feeds thread context into `createAcpDispatchDeliveryCoordinator`). The `dispatch-from-config.ts` call site is intentionally not changed — it has a comment warning that reading from `deliveryContext` can resurrect stale routes.

## Evidence

### Existing tests pass
```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts --no-coverage
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --no-coverage -t "thread"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed | 217 skipped (223)
```

### Source-level proof
The `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId("uses session key thread id suffix")` test continues to pass — the new parameter is optional and doesn't affect existing callers.

The gateway already writes `deliveryContext.threadId` during ACP spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`, via `requestDeliveryHint`). The fix simply reads what's already there through the same `string | number` path used by the existing `MessageThreadId`/`TransportThreadId` fallbacks.

### Not tested
- End-to-end ACP Discord delivery with a real Discord channel (requires live Discord + agent infrastructure)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- Adds one optional parameter (no existing callers broken)
- Reads `deliveryContext?.threadId` from an already-loaded session entry (no new IO)
- Uses the same `string | number` type already supported by the function

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
